question.text: A put option is Out of the Money (OTM) when:

answer.text: Spot price of underlying > Strike price
Spot price of underlying < Strike price
Spot price of underlying = Strike price

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Put OTM: Spot > Strike (no benefit from exercising the right to sell at a lower price than market). Call OTM: Spot < Strike. **The Trap:** For CALL options, OTM is Spot < Strike (opposite of puts). **Cross-Exam Context:** Moneyness is tested in Series I and VIII. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** OTM options have zero intrinsic value — their entire premium is time value.
